meta_title: For Freelancers: analytics setup to Solve stuck with basic apps (Intermediate Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome stuck with basic apps. A Intermediate-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.
h2_structure: The Problem: stuck with basic apps

content.seo_title: Analytics Setup for Freelancers: Scale Your AI Apps Fast
content.seo_description: Stop building blind. Learn how to implement high-performance analytics to solve app stagnation. Master Antigravity and Claude Code for professional output.
content.answer_block: To break through the 'basic app' ceiling, you must transition from feature-building to data-driven optimization. By implementing event-based analytics using Antigravity and Claude Code, you gain precise visibility into user friction points, allowing you to iterate on high-impact features that actually drive client ROI and retention.
content.intro:
You’ve shipped the MVP, but the metrics are a ghost town. Your client is asking for growth, yet you’re stuck manually guessing which features users actually touch. It’s the classic freelance trap: building more code without knowing if it moves the needle.

Real-world production apps aren't just code—they are feedback loops. If you aren't measuring user behavior, you aren't building a business; you’re just writing scripts. It’s time to stop guessing and start engineering with precision.

steps.title: Define Your North Star Metrics
steps.description: Identify the three critical events that define user success in your client's app before touching any code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current project architecture and suggest a tracking schema for 5 core user events that align with high-retention SaaS metrics.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Audit the codebase for key user interaction points and propose a JSON event schema.'

steps.title: Inject Analytics Middleware
steps.description: Create a decoupled middleware layer to handle event dispatching without cluttering your business logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a robust, type-safe analytics middleware template for my current tech stack that supports asynchronous batching.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a new analytics middleware file and integrate it into the main app entry point.'

steps.title: Implement Auto-Instrumentation
steps.description: Automate the tracking of UI interactions to save hours of manual event binding.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a HOC (Higher-Order Component) or wrapper that automatically instruments button clicks and form submissions.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Apply the tracking wrapper to all components inside the /features directory.'

steps.title: Establish Error-Boundary Tracking
steps.description: Don't just track successes; track where your users get frustrated by crashes or failed API calls.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create an error boundary handler that sends stack traces and user context to my analytics provider.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement global error boundary tracking and link it to the analytics service.'

steps.title: Validate Data Pipeline Integrity
steps.description: Ensure your events are actually firing correctly in the production environment.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a test script that simulates user sessions and verifies event delivery to the analytics dashboard.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a test suite to validate end-to-end event transmission.'

pro_tips: Never track everything. Focus on 'Actionable Events' that correlate directly to revenue or churn.
Use a 'Data Layer' abstraction so you can swap analytics providers without refactoring your entire codebase.
Always include a 'User ID' hash to track long-term cohort behavior without violating PII regulations.
Implement a 'Debug Mode' flag that logs events to the browser console for instant local development feedback.
Set up automated alerts for unexpected dips in your North Star metric so you can fix issues before the client notices.
content.next_steps: Review your data dashboard after 48 hours, identify the feature with the highest drop-off rate, and use Claude Code to perform an A/B test on that specific component.
content.conversion_block: Stop building in the dark and start shipping data-backed value that keeps clients coming back. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
